topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper felt like a physical assault. Leo stared at the 'D-' circled in thick black marker, his jaw tightening until a dull ache radiated through his temples. He slowly folded the sheet, the sound of the paper rustling under his touch sounding offensively loud in the quiet library corner. He didn't bother to look up when Ms. Albright finally cleared her throat, the sound grating on his nerves. "Leo, perhaps we can discuss the rubric later." Leo just nodded once, a jerky, minimal movement, and shoved the paper deep into his worn backpack, the zipper pulling tight against his chest. He walked out into the hallway, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead seeming to mock his failure. Every step felt heavy, weighted down by the sheer, crushing weight of that disappointing number.

[story 2]

"So, that's it?" Sarah muttered, the words tasting like ash in her mouth. She slammed the textbook shut, the impact echoing sharply in the lecture hall. She could feel the heat rising in her cheeks, a flush that had nothing to do with the room temperature. She picked up her pen, but her hand trembled, making the ink skip across the page. She looked down at the abysmal score, a strange, cold emptiness settling in her stomach. *Of course,* she thought, the realization sharp and unwelcome. *I tried. I really did.* She didn't bother to make eye contact with anyone, focusing instead on the intricate pattern of the desk surface, wishing she could just dissolve into the cheap laminate.

[story 3]

Mark traced the lines of the feedback with a fingernail, the cheap plastic of the grading sheet offering no comfort. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped him, ragged and shallow. He stood by the window, watching the rain streak down the glass, each drop mirroring the slow, steady erosion of his confidence. He didn't speak to anyone; the silence was more potent than any accusation. He clenched his fists until his knuckles shone white, a silent protest against the unfairness of the assessment. He turned away from the window, the movement stiff, and began walking towards the exit, his pace measured and heavy, determined to carry the unpleasantness with him out into the indifferent afternoon.
